prompt: A Hospitality Operations Management System that includes end-to-end event management for corporate catering and venue services, combining planning, content, and resource control into a single on-prem application. Users sign in with a local username and password and work through role-based screens built in Svelte, optimized for tablet check-in and back-office desktop use. Event Planners create events with dates, service windows, headcount, venue/resource requests, a materials list, and a budget that enforces a default cap of $25,000.00 unless an Approver explicitly overrides it. Each event follows a visible state machine (Draft, Submitted, Approved, In Service, Closed) with key-node approvals for budget changes above 10% and for any special resources; users see timeline-style process logs and can attach contracts, menus, and photos with immediate upload validation and clear status feedback. Resource Managers handle a reservation/checkout workflow that closes the loop from request to approval to release, occupancy, and return, including cancellation, rescheduling, and renewals; overtime past 30 minutes triggers a mandatory justification and an additional approval step, and over-quota requests require a policy exception note. Culinary Editors maintain recipe data with structured steps, quantities, difficulty, time estimates, tags, and rich text with images; recipes support drafts, version revisions, and a review status workflow so only approved versions can be used in event materials lists. Inventory Analysts view price and inventory snapshots over time for ingredients and rentals, with UI indicators for anomalous swings and missing-day gaps that require correction before reporting. The backend uses Fastify to provide a decoupled REST-style API consumed by the Svelte frontend, with PostgreSQL as the system of record for events, approvals, reservations, entitlements, recipes, catalog metadata, and immutable audit trails. The resource catalog is modeled as a unified tree with configurable resource types and metadata templates that include required fields, validation rules, versioning, publish/unpublish status, tagging, and relationship maintenance to internal training references such as resource-course, grade level, and subject area used for staff enablement. Entitlements are issued automatically (for example, "10 staff meals per event role" on approval), manually, or via offline bulk import; redemption is idempotent, blocks expired or insufficient quota, supports rollback on failure, and writes fully traceable redemption records linked to the event and user. Attachments are archived to local storage with SHA-256 fingerprints, a 25 MB per-file limit, and allowlisted formats; sensitive fields (e.g., employee IDs) are masked in UI and encrypted at rest using a locally managed key. Anti-crawling and stability mechanisms are implemented as an internal data-collection subsystem designed to run without internet dependency: it supports rotating proxy definitions for controlled environments, dynamic user agents, cookie/session handling, redirect parsing, health checks, and graceful degradation to queued/manual review; CAPTCHA integration is exposed only as a local plug-in interface and is not required for core operation. High availability is achieved via local caching and cache invalidation policies, adaptive on-box image resizing/transcoding, optional SSR for critical screens, circuit breaking on slow subsystems, and scheduled backups with quarterly disaster recovery drills validated by restore tests.
